mnemonics: Flip-Flops Feel Loyal (to the clock) - Unlike Latches, who are Lazy (level-sensitive).
keyPoints: Sequential logic has memory; its output depends on current inputs AND past states.
Synchronous circuits use a clock signal to coordinate state changes.
The basic storage element is a flip-flop, which is edge-triggered.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to initialize flip-flops to a known state (reset), leading to unpredictable behavior.
Creating unintentional latches in combinational logic by not defining all possible input cases in HDL.
